LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PETER KHOURY AND H324 LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE 10TH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE ELEMENTS OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION OF CONVERSION ARE ADEQUATELY PLEADED IN THE COMPLAINT. THE ALLEGATIONS ARE NOT UNCERTAIN. THE COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANT CONVERTED THE DOMAIN NAME. SEE CRS RECOVERY, INC. V. LAXTON, (9 TH CIR. 2010) 600 F.3D 1138, 1142. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PETER KHOURY AND H324 LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE ATTORNEYS FEES AND PUNITIVE AND EXEMPLARY DAMAGES AND CERTAIN OTHER ALLEGATIONS IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE MINI MINUTES AND ORDER FOR DETAILS OF THE RULING.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PETER KHOURY AND H324 LLC'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE MOTION TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS TIMELY MADE AND GRANTING THE MOTION WILL NOT PREJUDICE CROSS-DEFENDANT. THE TWO NEW CAUSES OF ACTION ARISE FROM THE SAME FACTS SET FORTH IN THE ORIGINAL CROSS-COMPLAINT. CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS ALREADY AGREED TO A FURTHER DEPOSITION. CROSS-DEFENDANT HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT THE PROPOSED AMENDMENT IS BEING MADE IN BAD FAITH. THE ATTACHED DISCOVERY RESPONSES DO NOT FORECLOSE FILING THE PROPOSED AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Attendance At Deposition And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-09-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS PETER KHOURY and H324 LLC'S Motion To Compel Attendance At Deposition And For Monetary Sanctions is Off calendar per parties' agreement. A Stipulation and Order was signed prior to the hearing. Judge Pro Tem: Robert Kane; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended X-Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS PETER KHOURY AND H324 LLC'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross-Complaint IS Granted. The motion to file a first amended cross-complaint is timely made and granting the motion will not prejudice Cross-Defendant. The two new causes of action arise from the same facts set forth in the original cross-complaint. Cross-Complainant has already agreed to a further deposition. Cross-Defendant has not established that the proposed amendment is being made in bad faith. The attached discovery responses do not foreclose filing the proposed amended cross-complaint. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-20-2013 Set For Trial On Jan-27-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-02-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ELLEN P. LIU, ESQ., 415.392.1960, FOR PLAINTIFF ALAN OVSON dba OVSEN COMMUNICATIONS dba METHODSPEAKING; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS PETER KHOURY and H324 LLC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled as to the 10th cause of action. The elements of the cause of action of conversion are adequately pleaded in the complaint. The allegations are not uncertain. The complaint sufficiently alleges that defendant converted the domain name. See CRS Recovery, Inc. v. Laxton, (9 th Cir. 2010) 600 F.3d 1138, 1142. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Notice Ofmotion And Omtion To Strike Attorneys Fees And Punitive And Exemplary Damages Adn Certain Other Allegations
Off Calendar May-02-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ELLEN P. LIU, ESQ., 415.392.1960, FOR PLAINTIFF ALAN OVSON dba OVSEN COMMUNICATIONS dba METHODSPEAKING; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS PETER KHOURY and H324 LLC's Motion To Strike Attorneys Fees And Punitive And Exemplary Damages And Certain Other Allegations is denied. The only basis for fees that Plaintiff cites in opposition to the motion to strike is Civil Procedure Code section 1021.5. However, Plaintiff is not even required to plead his intent to seek attorneys' fees under CCP 1021.5. Snatchko v. Westfield LLC (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 469, 497. The Court thus denies the motion to strike this requested relief at the pleading stage. Finally, Defendant states in Reply that, despite the reference in the caption of his motion to strike to "punitive and exemplary damages," he was not moving to strike references to these damages. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
